widgets_values: cinematic 3D animated video, production quality, stable identity, consistent character appearance, detailed materials and textures. Long-auto segmented render: keep this segment visually continuous with its first frame, follow local prompts and the selected camera motion for this segment only.

nodes.title: LONG AUTO DIRECTOR - Segment Timeline Source
Long-Auto Notes
widgets_values:
# Shezw Long-Auto: segmented long-video render template

Purpose: render long Director timelines as ordered segments, with tail-frame continuity between segments.

This workflow keeps the same core chain as Pro Console: Director timeline, keyframes, references, local prompts, camera track, optional camera/motion IC video, video output, and tail-frame PNG output.

Long-auto render logic:
- Full timeline can be 30s/60s+ in the Director UI.
- Segment planner cuts at manual cuts first. When Auto Cut is ON, camera and IC-Control boundaries also become split points. Optional max segment length is disabled by default.
- Manual cuts have a 0.25s protection zone by default; camera/IC auto cuts inside that zone are ignored and timeline items are snapped to the manual cut while materializing each segment.
- Queue All Cuts renders segments sequentially, waits for each segment to finish, reads its tail-frame PNG from ComfyUI history, then feeds it into the next segment.
- If a segment starts with an explicit keyframe, use that keyframe as first frame.
- If no start keyframe exists, use the previous segment tail-frame PNG as the next segment first frame.
- Text/camera/static tracks are clipped into per-segment copies. Control video tracks are frame-sliced with trimStart/length per segment.
- If a tail-frame PNG cannot be found before the next segment, rendering stops instead of continuing with a random first frame.
- Each segment writes its own video and tail frame.
- Final video can be built by concatenating segment videos and muxing the original full audio when needed.

Video IC-Control is automatic and optional: leave optional camera/action video paths empty to skip them; provide a video path and add an IC-Control track segment with strength > 0 to enable the corresponding guide.

Outputs:
- Segment video prefix: `video/long-auto-segment`
- Tail-frame PNG prefix: `video/long-auto-tail-frame`

Important: Do not use this as a single-pass 30s LTX generation graph. Press Queue/Queue All Cuts to run sequential segmented renders, or keep activeSegmentIndex set for a single segment test.

nodes.title: OPTIONAL AUTO CAMERA CONTROL VIDEO / IMAGE INPUT
GET LAST FRAME AS PNG SOURCE
OPTIONAL AUTO MOTION / ACTION CONTROL VIDEO INPUT
